reason: Natural Born Killers – savage media critique in a darkly comic spree
Wag the Dog – political spin and propaganda exposed with sharp humor
Dr. Strangelove – biting Cold War satire on military-industrial absurdity
Brazil – hyper-bureaucratic dystopia lampoons corporate control
Pulp Fiction – pop culture and crime collide in edgy, self-aware satire
Glengarry Glen Ross – ruthless corporate sales world laid bare with dark wit
Fear of a Black Hat – mockumentary skewers gangsta rap tropes with comedy
Enchanted April – upper-class retreat reveals social pretensions in satiric tone
Saint, The – rogue spy comedy skewers pop-culture and ideology
Canadian Bacon – cross-border caricature of media and military follies
